title: Protein expression of hard-to-produce proteins in the cytoplasm of Escherichia coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fneee","text":"This protocol describes the expression and extraction of heterologously cytoplasmically expressed proteins in the bacterium E.coli, for T7 based expression systems. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":124,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3senj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b1uk5","text":"Specifically, it describes how to produce \"hard-to-produce\", as for example, LPMOs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"79n0q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c4t32","text":"It is based on a modified version of:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"88o0b","text":"\"IPTG Induction and  Extraction of Proteins from Bacteria\" by Swathi Arur and Sudhir Nayak, Schedl Lab. Biol.10, 122–126.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4u7a7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a62e9","text":"The extraction of cytoplasmatic proteins is followed by a chemical lysis protocol, alternatively, sonication can also be employed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ebg2v","text":"Make sure to clone your favorite gene for expression in the appropriate expression vector. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bk1od","text":"Prepare a fresh transformation of your expression vector in E. coli BL21 (DE3) cells or other appropriate strain (e.g. sHuffle strain, Rosetta, etc.)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":60,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pick a fresh colony of your BL21 (DE3) strain previously transformed with your expression vector, and inoculate it in LB medium supplemented with relevant antibiotics. Grow the culture at 37 °C at 250 RPM shaking Overnight . The volume of the overnight culture depends on the volume of the expression culture and should be at least 1/100 of the expression culture
Dilute the overnight culture 1:100 in fresh LB supplemented with relevant antibiotics
Collect induced samples by centrifugation at 8000 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes and carefully discard the supernatant Pellets can be frozen here at -80 °C or -20 °C until protein extraction
Grow the culture at 37 °C with 250 RPM shaking until an OD 600 = 0.5 - 0.6
Induce the expression by adding IPTG to a final concentration of 1 millimolar (mM)
Grow the culture for 20:00:00 at 18 °C with 180 RPM shacking. The temperature can be lowered further allowing for a slower induction in order to increase the production of soluble product.
Lyse the cells using a lysis protocol or a commercially available kit
Keep the extractions On ice when working with it and at 4 °C for storage
Move the culture into an incubator set to 18 °C with 180 RPM of shaking and allow the culture to reach OD 600 = 0.8 - 1.0
creator: Cristina Hernandez Rollan
